rationale.rule: When every sampled entity in a definition is a closed LWPOLYLINE on a layer literally named for a material fill (INSUL/HATCH-type suffix), classify it as a floor-plan material component rather than wall geometry, since wall runs are normally drawn as open 2-point LINE segments or parallel line pairs, not small closed polygons.
rationale.counterfactual: If the layer name instead used a structural/wall naming convention (e.g. 벽체, WALL) or if the polylines were open 2-vertex segments chained end-to-end into a long run, the wall_likelihood judgment would rise substantially and some entities would qualify as wall_line_handles.
rationale.uncertainty: The projection gives no vertex coordinates or bbox for LWPOLYLINE entities (bbox is only derived from LINE start/end), so the actual size, shape, and spatial adjacency of these quads to any real wall centerline cannot be confirmed from this data alone.

rationale.evidence: 23 entities: 1 INSERT of the nested block X-평면도(기본형)$0$국기계양대 (flagpole) plus 20 axis-aligned LINE segments forming a stepped rectangular frame with bbox (-560,-103.5)-(560,22.5), and 2 WIPEOUT entities (layer 0) that mask background hatching behind the symbol; the frame's longest straight segments (4858, 485A, 4868) each span ~1076 units and are duplicated/coincident pairs, a pattern typical of a small curb or plinth outline rather than a building wall run.
rationale.rule: A block that INSERTs a named site-fixture block (here 국기계양대=flagpole) and pairs it with WIPEOUT masking is a discrete plan symbol; even when it contains long straight rectilinear LINE segments, those should be read as the fixture's own footprint/plinth outline rather than building wall lines, especially when the overall footprint (roughly 1076x126 drawing units, i.e. sub-meter to ~1m scale) is far smaller than a typical wall run.
rationale.counterfactual: If the INSERT referenced a wall/structural block instead of 국기계양대, or if these frame lines connected to other wall-scaled runs beyond this definition's small footprint, the wall_likelihood would need to be raised and the long lines reclassified as genuine wall candidates.
rationale.uncertainty: Without the parent layout's full geometry it cannot be confirmed whether this stepped frame abuts an actual building wall (e.g. a flagpole set into a wall niche) or sits freestanding in open ground, so a small residual wall_likelihood is kept for the longest duplicated lines rather than zero.
